title: Hard GPU Reset on quit in minecraft 26.2 with VulkanAPI
author: LazySeldi
body:
This mod causes crashes when using minecraft's native Vulkan API on 26.2
At least for me.
My setups is:
Arch Linux
Prism Launcher(unmodified modpack straight from modrinth)
Hyprland
AMD Mesa Drivers

This is not just any crash, this for me at least is a hard crash, like whole GPU VRAM resets.
The GPU stalls for some time and then the GPU reset is initiated by the kernel.
When using the OpenGL API it doesn't crash when quitting.
No error logs are shown at all. This happens when you quit that's it.

title: [All] [Forge] Improperly formatted mods.toml
author: Fealtous
body:
`[[mixins]]` and `[[accessTransformers]]` are not valid toml headers on forge. We do offer the field `accessTransformers` to allow for (multiple) toml defined transformers in 47.4.20 or newer, though it hasn't been backported further than 1.20.1 as of now. All relevant versions of forge already inspects the path `META-INF/accesstransformer.cfg` so you don't technically need to define it in your toml at all if you're not using a custom name or location.

`[[mixins]]` is not a valid header or field in any version, however. As far as I am aware it is neo exclusive.

This issue appears to affect all listed versions. The `[[accessTransformers]]` header would have caused a crash, but it is such a common mistake that versions 1.21.11 and under include a failsafe.

I can write up a series of PRs to fix all of them if you wish.

title: [1.21.2-1.21.4] [Forge] Invalid mods.toml due to typo
author: Fealtous
body:
https://github.com/juliand665/Dynamic-FPS/blob/20c87892a9a29585181215c3d04e1e8c42fd099a/platforms/forge/src/main/resources/META-INF/mods.toml#L95
Due to a dot turned comma this fails to parse and crashes the game.

title: Wayland + NVIDIA PRIME render offload: window permanently stuck in "unfocused" state, FPS capped at 30
author: Mooling0602
body:
### Describe the bug

When running Minecraft on Linux with Wayland and NVIDIA PRIME render offload (integrated Intel GPU for display, discrete NVIDIA GPU for rendering), the mod incorrectly detects the window as "unfocused" and permanently caps the frame rate at 30 FPS, even when the window is focused and actively being used.

The FPS returns to normal (~175 FPS) immediately after disabling the mod.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Linux with Wayland compositor (tested on niri 26.04, wlroots-based)
2. Intel iGPU (display) + NVIDIA dGPU (PRIME render offload), e.g. `__NV_PRIME_RENDER_OFFLOAD=1`
3. Minecraft 26.1.2 with Fabric loader
4. Install only `dynamic-fps` (3.11.7), or a modpack that includes it (tested with Fabulously Optimized 13.2.2)
5. Default config: `unfocused.frame_rate_target = 30`
6. Launch the game with discrete GPU
7. Observe FPS is locked at 30 regardless of window focus

### Expected behavior

When the Minecraft window is focused and actively being played, FPS should not be limited by the "unfocused" power state.

### Root cause analysis

> The analyzation haven't verified yet, it's from LLM's output. But links are avaliable.

This appears to be a circular dependency between GLFW/Wayland and the mod's focus detection:

1. **Wayland compositors require the client to render at least one frame** before granting keyboard focus to the window. This is by design — the compositor needs a committed buffer to associate input events with.
2. **Dynamic-FPS checks the window focus state** and, when the window is "unfocused", applies `frame_rate_target = 30` and skips full-rate rendering.
3. With **NVIDIA PRIME render offload**, the cross-GPU DMA-BUF transfer adds additional latency to the first frame submission, making it even harder to break out of this cycle.

The result: the window never gets properly "focused" from the mod's perspective → stuck at 30 FPS forever. This does **not** happen when rendering directly on the Intel iGPU (without PRIME offload), because the first frame can be submitted quickly enough to satisfy the compositor's focus requirement.

See also: [Arseny Kapoulkine's analysis of the GLFW Wayland focus deadlock](https://mastodon.gamedev.place/@zeux/116105923982053448), [NVIDIA egl-wayland PRIME offload issues](https://github.com/NVIDIA/egl-wayland/issues/101).

### Workaround

Disable the mod, or set `unfocused.frame_rate_target` to `-1` (disabled) in `dynamic_fps.json`:

```json
{
  "states": {
    "unfocused": {
      "frame_rate_target": -1,
      "run_garbage_collector": true
    }
  }
}
```

### Environment

- **OS**: NixOS (unstable, Linux 7.0.11)
- **Compositor**: niri 26.04 (wlroots-based Wayland compositor)
- **GPU**: Intel Alder Lake iGPU (PCI:0:2:0) + NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3050 Laptop (PCI:1:0:0)
- **NVIDIA driver**: 610.43.02 (open kernel module), PRIME render offload with `nvidia-drm.modeset=1`
- **Minecraft**: 26.1.2, Fabric loader
- **Dynamic FPS version**: 3.11.7

### Additional context

- This issue was drafted with the assistance of a large language model (DeepSeek V4 Pro, via Claude Code CLI) and has been reviewed and confirmed by a human user. AI assistance was used because the reporter is not a native English speaker.

- Using [BlazeSDL](https://modrinth.com/mod/blazesdl) to replace GLFW with SDL3, and set `SDL_VIDEODRIVER=wayland` to run it in Wayland mode.

title: Memory Leak with the Wynncraft Plus modpack + Distant Horizons
author: PlayfulPiano
body:
Minecraft 1.21.11
DFPS version 3.11.6
Wynncraft Plus v2.2.2 Modpack (Replaced Voxy with DH 2.4.5-b)

Was noticing huge memory leaks leading to a maxed out RAM usage on my PC (32GB total), ending up with minecraft crashing out of nowhere. When tabbing back into minecraft, the memory usage dropped substantially and stabilized. If I tabbed out, after a few moments it started to climb again (albeit not always consistently). This was me using Java 25 w/COH but only 5GB allocated, so it even reaching 10GB+ is abnormal.

My PC specs are a 7800x3d CPU and an R7 9070 GPU, using Linux Mint Cinnamon 22.3.

title: Afk mode
author: maartix1
body: If a player is afk for 10 or 15 minutes it reduces fps, this can be useful for people who are afk at night.

title: Dynamic FPS with Unlimited FPS crashes Intel graphics driver when Minecraft is minimized
author: ghostwycloud
body:
### Bug Report

In version 26.1.2, using Dynamic FPS with Unlimited FPS causes the Intel graphics driver to crash.

This happens on Intel driver version 8801 and newer.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Enable Dynamic FPS
2. Launch Minecraft
3. Set FPS to Unlimited
4. Minimize Minecraft or leave it running in the background
5. Intel graphics driver crashes

### Expected Behavior

Minecraft should keep running normally in the background.

### Actual Behavior

The Intel graphics driver crashes when Minecraft is minimized with Dynamic FPS enabled and FPS set to Unlimited.

### Additional Information

* Affected Intel driver versions: 8801 and newer
* Tested version: 26.1.2


https://streamable.com/povlw0
